# System Requirements Specification — Hypothetical ECU

Version 1.2 (approved)

## 1 Specify and structure system requirements

This document specifies the functional and non-functional system requirements
derived from the stakeholder requirements. Requirements are documented
according to defined characteristics (verifiable, unambiguous, free from
design/implementation, non-contradicting).

- REQ-SYS-001: The ECU shall respond to a CAN request within 50 ms (approved).
- REQ-SYS-002: The ECU shall operate between -40°C and +125°C (approved).
- REQ-SYS-003: Non-functional — startup time shall be < 200 ms (approved).

## 2 Prioritize system requirements

Requirements are grouped by functional area and prioritized for the Release
1.2 scope. Priorities are tracked in `requirement attribute` fields.

## 3 Analyze system requirements

Analysis results for technical feasibility and interdependency are captured
in Analysis_Results.xlsx. Impact on the operating environment was evaluated
jointly with system architecture.

## 4 Consistency and bidirectional traceability

A traceability matrix establishes bidirectional traceability between every
system requirement and the upstream stakeholder requirement. Consistency
evidence is captured in the review record Consistency_Review_2026-03.md.

## 5 Communicate agreed system requirements

Communication evidence: requirements walkthrough minutes were distributed to
all affected parties (system architects, software team, hardware team,
validation team, quality assurance).
